brief: Today is Push A — chest, shoulders, triceps. You read fully recovered: no pain notes, clean rest between sets, and your last three days (push, pull, legs) all landed strong, so we step forward across the board. Incline: your two lighter sets go up 5 lb (140, then 160) because you hit the rep ceiling on both last time; the top two stay at 165 and 170 but I want one more rep each. Machine chest press: you already beat 157.5 for 10, so the top set steps to 165. Two honesty fixes where the app was about to overstate you — cable lateral raises are one real push set at 10.5 then 7.5 for the back-offs (last time you dropped to 7.5 after the first set), and overhead triceps tops at 54x11 (you got 54 for 10, not 12). Rope pushdowns step to 60.5 and face pulls to 27.5 — both were maxed on reps, so weight is the only way up. Everything else, bank an extra rep or two. Conditioning: you did 15 min easy elliptical on the 21st — get another 15-20 min of easy cardio in today or tomorrow to keep the cut moving toward March. Two structure notes for when you are at a computer (NOT tonight's numbers): your Push A vs Push B chest-press rep-range labels look backwards — Push B is really your heavier chest day — and overhead triceps is buried dead-last on Push A. Both are worth a tidy-up in a Claude session; I cannot change the plan myself.

flags.note: Two lifts today (cable lateral raise, overhead triceps) sit a touch lower than the app first suggested — those match what you actually hit last time, not an inflated repeat.

overrides.reason: You hit the 8-rep ceiling on both lighter sets last time, so +5 lb each; the top two stay put but earn one more rep before adding weight.
You already cleared 157.5 for 10 on Push B, so the top set steps to the next notch, 165, instead of handing back what you beat.
Weight is right but you were sitting at the 12-rep floor, so bank reps toward the middle of the range this time.
Mid-range at 8/8/9 in an 8-10 slot, so hold 35 and push each set one rep toward 10 before the dumbbells jump to 40.
Last time you only got 10.5 for 10 then dropped to 7.5 — so one honest push set at 10.5 and 7.5 back-offs, not 10.5 three times.
You got 54 for 10 last time, not 12, so the top set is a reachable 54x11 with 52.5 as the ramp — no inflated target.
You hit the 12-rep ceiling at 57.5, so step to the next loadable notch, 60.5, and let reps settle back into range.
You cleared both fixed-15 sets at 25.5 two days ago, so on a rep-capped slot the only way up is weight — go to 27.5.

proposals.why: Your two chest-press prescriptions are backwards. Push A is labelled the heavy 8-10 range, but the machine sits behind four sets of incline there, so you pressed 150x10. Push B is labelled the lighter 10-12 and you pressed 157.5x10 — heavier, on the day meant to be lighter. Flip the labels so Push A is the 10-12 exposure and Push B the heavy 8-10. (Re-surfaced from yesterday; still unactioned.)
Same fix — make Push B the heavy 8-10 chest exposure, since that is where your chest is actually fresh and you press heavier.

The same lift is two different athletes across the split: 54x10 for three sets on Push B (fresh) versus only 47.5x13 for two sets on Push A, where it sits sixth after both presses, the crossover, the shoulder press and the lateral raise. Move it ahead of the Push A lateral raise so its one Push A exposure starts on fresh arms instead of the dregs.

proposals_note: Two structure proposals from tonight's program seat. Neither moved a number tonight — they are yours to approve or reject in a Claude session. The chest-press label flip repeats yesterday's still-open recommendation.
